title: erigeron vicarius
intro: Erigeron vicarius, also known as Poverty Grass or Western Sea Blush, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Erigeron vicarius include its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, making it a popular remedy for minor injuries and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, salve, or tincture, which can be used topically to treat skin issues or ingested to support overall health and well-being.
